**task-profile** when:\n45\t- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n46\t- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n47\t- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n48\t- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n49\t- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n50\t- Logic\n55\t\n56\tUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n57\t- Look for direct project name matches\n58\t- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n59\t- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n60\t- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n61\t\n62\t## Segmentation Guidelines\n63\t\n64\t- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n65\t- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n66\t- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n67\t\n68\t## Confidence Threshold\n69\t\n70\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n71\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n72\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n73\t\n74\t## Status Intent Notes\n75\t\n76\t- call).\"\"\"\n118\t        try:\n119\t            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n120\t        except Exception as e:\n121\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n122\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n123\t\n124\t    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n125\t        \"\"\"\n126\t        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n127\t\n128\t        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n129\t        src/components/hot_reload.py). Returns \"\" on failure\n131\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n132\t        \"\"\"\n133\t        try:\n134\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n135\t        except Exception as e:\n136\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n137\t            return \"\"\n138\t\n139\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n140\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n141\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n142\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n143\t        if urgency_prompt:\n144\t            system_prompt = (\n145\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n146\t            )\n147\t        return system_prompt\n148\t\n149\t    async def classify_utterance(\n150\t        self,\n151\t        utterance: str,\n152\t        session_id: str,\n153\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n154\t        \"\"\"\n155\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n156\t\n157\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n158\t\n159\t        Args:\n160\t            utterance: The user utterance\n161\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n162\t\n163\t        Returns:\n164\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n165\t        \"\"\"\n166\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n167\t\n168\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n169\t        store = await self._get_store()\n170\t        session_context = \"\"\n171\t        try:\n172\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n173\t            if session:\n174\t                # Get recent intents for context\n175\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n176\t                if recent_intents:\n177\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n178\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n179\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n180\t        except Exception as e:\n181\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n182\t\n183\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n184\t\n185\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n186\t\n187\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n188\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n189\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n190\t\n191\t        try:\n192\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n193\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n194\t                user_message=user_message,\n195\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n196\t                max_tokens=2048,\n197\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n198\t            )\n199\t\n200\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n201\t            raw = response.strip()\n202\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n203\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n204\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n205\t\n206\t            # Parse JSON response\n207\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n208\t            classifications = []\n209\t\n210\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n211\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n212\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n213\t                try:\n214\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n215\t                except ValueError:\n216\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n217\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n218\t\n219\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n220\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n221\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n222\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n223\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n224\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n225\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n226\t                )\n227\t                classifications.append(classification)\n228\t\n229\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n230\t            return classifications\n231\t\n232\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n233\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n234\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n267\t        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n268\t        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n269\t        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n270\t        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n271\t        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n272\t\n273\t        routed_intents = []\n274\t        for classification in classifications:\n275\t            # Create intent ID\n276\t            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n277\t\n278\t            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n279\t                intent_id=intent_id,\n280\t                classification=classification,\n281\t                session_id=session_id,\n282\t                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n283\t                router_ms=router_ms,\n284\t            )\n285\t            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n286\t\n287\t        return routed_intents\n288\t\n289\t    async def process_intent(\n290\t        self,\n291\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n292\t    ) -> dict:\n293\t        \"\"\"\n294\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n295\t\n296\t        Args:\n297\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n298\t\n299\t        Returns:\n300\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n301\t        \"\"\"\n302\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n303\t\n304\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n305\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n306\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n307\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n308\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n309\t        # the dispatch itself.\n310\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n311\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n312\t\n313\t        try:\n314\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n315\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n316\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n317\t            else:\n318\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n319\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n320\t        except Exception:\n321\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n322\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n323\t            raise\n324\t\n325\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n326\t        return result\n327\t\n328\t    async def _persist_timings(\n329\t        self,\n330\t        intent_id: str,\n331\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n332\t    ) -> None:\n333\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. except Exception as e:\n338\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n339\t\n340\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n341\t        self,\n342\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n343\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n344\t    ) -> dict:\n345\t        \"\"\"\n346\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n347\t\n348\t                ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n378\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n379\t                    location = entry.display_path\n380\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved   )\n408\t\n409\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n410\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n411\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n412\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n413\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n414\t\n415\t            def           )\n491\t\n492\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n493\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n494\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n495\t FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n604\t\n605\t    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n606\t        self,\n607\t        bead_ref: str,\n608\t    ) -> dict | None:\n609\t        \"\"\"\n610\t        Check if a            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n630\t\n631\t            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n632\t                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n633\t {e}\")\n769\t            return {\n770\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n771\t                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n772\t                \"status\": \"error\",\n773\t                \"error\": str(e),\n774\t                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n775\t            }\n776\t\n777\t    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n778\t
