NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n100\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n101\t    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n102\t    acked_at    INTEGER,\n103\t    previous_result_id TEXT,  -- Link to previous result for diff\n104\t    diff_summary TEXT,  -- Human-readable diff summary\n105\t    diff_data    TEXT,  -- JSON: detailed field diffs\n106\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n107\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n108\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n109\t    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n110\t);\n111\t\n112\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_session ON results(session_id);\n113\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_topic ON results(topic_id);\n114\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_created ON results(created_at);\n115\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_results_previous ON results(previous_result_id);\n116\t\n117\t-- Topics: persistent concerns organizing intents and results\n118\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topics (\n119\t    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n120\t    label        TEXT NOT NULL,\n121\t    type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n122\t    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n123\t    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', 'global')) DEFAULT 'session',\n124\t    session_id   TEXT,\n125\t    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n126\t    last_active  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n127\t    archived_at  INTEGER,\n128\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n129\t);\n130\t\n131\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_session ON topics(session_id);\n132\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_scope ON topics(scope);\n133\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_topics_active ON topics(last_active DESC);\n134\t\n135\t-- Topic context cache: pre-warmed context for active topics\n136\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topic_context_cache (\n137\t    topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n138\t    context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n139\t    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n140\t    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n141\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n142\t);\n143\t\n144\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n145\t\n146\t-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\n147\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n148\t    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n149\t    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n150\t    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n151\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n152\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n153\t);\n154\t\n155\t-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\n156\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n157\t    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n158\t    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n159\t    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n160\t    result_id    TEXT,\n161\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n162\t    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n163\t    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n164\t    surface_type TEXT,\n165\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n166\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n167\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n168\t    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n169\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n170\t);\n171\t\n172\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n173\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n174\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n175\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n176\t\n177\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n178\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n179\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n180\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\n223\t-- Card cache: pre-rendered HTML for result components\n224\t-- Stores server-side rendered HTML for result/component/layout combinations.\n225\t-- Primary key (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket) allows multiple cached\n226\t-- variations per result (e.g., different layouts). Populated after component\n227\t-- selection to avoid repeated rendering for the same result.\n228\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS card_cache (\n229\t    result_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n230\t    component_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n231\t    layout_bucket  TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t    rendered_html  TEXT NOT NULL,\n233\t    created_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n234\t    PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n235\t);\n236\t\n237\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_card_cache_result_id ON card_cache(result_id);\n238\t\"\"\"\n239\t\n240\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n241\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n242\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n243\t    \"router_ms\",\n244\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n245\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n246\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n247\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n248\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n249\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n250\t    \"stt_ms\",\n251\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n252\t)\n253\t\n254\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n255\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n256\t\n257\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n258\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n259\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n260\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n261\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n262\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n263\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n264\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n265\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n266\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n270\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n271\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n272\t\n273\t\n274\tclass SessionStore:\n275\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n276\t\n277\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n278\t        self.db_path = db_path\n279\t\n280\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n281\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n282\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n283\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n284\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n285\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n286\t\n287\t            # Create schema\n288\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n289\t            await db.commit()\n290\t\n291\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n292\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n293\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n294\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n295\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n296\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n297\t\n298\t    @staticmethod\n299\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n300\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n301\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n302\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n303\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n304\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n305\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n306\t            await db.execute(\n307\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n308\t            )\n309\t\n310\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n311\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n312\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n313\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n314\t\n315\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n316\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n317\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n318\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n319\t\n320\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n321\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n322\t\n323\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n324\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n325\t\n326\t        await db.commit()\n327\t\n328\t    @staticmethod\n329\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n330\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n331\t\n332\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n333\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n334\t        \"\"\"\n335\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n336\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n337\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n338\t            status_col = None\n339\t            for col in cols:\n340\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n341\t                    status_col = col\n342\t                    break\n343\t\n344\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n345\t        if not status_col:\n346\t            return\n347\t\n348\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n349\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n350\t        async with db.execute(\n351\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n352\t        ) as cur:\n353\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n354\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n355\t                # Already migrated\n356\t                return\n357\t\n358\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n359\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n360\t\n361\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n362\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n363\t\n364\t        try:\n365\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n366\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n367\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n368\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n369\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n370\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n371\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n372\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n373\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n374\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n375\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n376\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n377\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n378\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n379\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n380\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n381\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n382\t                )\n383\t            \"\"\")\n384\t\n385\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n386\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n387\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n388\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n389\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n390\t                FROM intents\n391\t            \"\"\")\n392\t\n393\t            # Recreate indexes\n394\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n395\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n396\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n397\t\n398\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n399\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n400\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n401\t\n402\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n403\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n404\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n405\t\n406\t            await db.commit()\n407\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n408\t\n409\t        except Exception as e:\n410\t            await db.rollback()\n411\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n412\t            raise\n413\t\n414\t    @staticmethod\n415\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n416\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n417\t\n418\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n419\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n420\t        \"\"\"\n421\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n422\t        async with db.execute(\n423\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n424\t        ) as cur:\n425\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n426\t            if not table_sql:\n427\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n428\t\n429\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n430\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n431\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n432\t                    # Already migrated\n433\t                    return\n434\t\n435\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n436\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n437\t\n438\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n439\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n440\t\n441\t        try:\n442\t            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n443\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n444\t                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n445\t                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n446\t                    intent_id   TEXT,\n447\t                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n448\t                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n449\t                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n450\t                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n451\t                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n452\t                    result_type TEXT,\n453\t                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n454\t                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n455\t                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n456\t                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n457\t                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n458\t                    diff_summary TEXT,\n459\t                    diff_data    TEXT,\n460\t                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n461\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n462\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n463\t                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n464\t                )\n465\t            \"\"\")\n466\t\n467\t            # Copy data from old table to new table - explicit column mapping\n468\t            # to handle different column orders between old and new schemas\n469\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n470\t                INSERT INTO results_new (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, result_type, card_fallback, created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n471\t                SELECT id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency,\n472\t                       COALESCE(result_type, 'default') AS result_type,\n473\t                       COALESCE(card_fallback, 0) AS card_fallback,\n474\t                       created_at, surfaced_at, acked_at, previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data\n475\t                FROM results\n476\t            \"\"\")\n477\t\n478\t            # Recreate indexes\n479\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n480\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n481\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n482\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n483\t\n484\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n485\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n486\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n487\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n488\t\n489\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n490\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n491\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n492\t\n493\t            await db.commit()\n494\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n495\t        except Exception as e:\n496\t            await db.rollback()\n497\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n498\t            raise\n499\t\n500\t    @staticmethod\n501\t    async def _migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n502\t        \"\"\"Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1.\n503\t\n504\t        The initial schema had DEFAULT 0, which meant the first comment (index 0)\n505\t        was skipped on the first check. With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n506\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n507\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n508\t        \"\"\"\n509\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n510\t        async with db.execute(\n511\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n512\t        ) as cur:\n513\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n514\t            if not table_sql:\n515\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n516\t\n517\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n518\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n519\t                # Already migrated\n520\t                return\n521\t\n522\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n523\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n524\t\n525\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n526\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n527\t\n528\t        try:\n529\t            # Create new table with DEFAULT -1\n530\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n531\t                CREATE TABLE bead_watch_new (\n532\t                    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n533\t                    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n534\t                    last_refusal_reason TEXT,\n535\t                    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,\n536\t                    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,\n537\t                    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n538\t                    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,\n539\t                    fenced_at          INTEGER,\n540\t                    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL\n541\t                )\n542\t            \"\"\")\n543\t\n544\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n545\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n546\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n547\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n548\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n549\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n550\t            \"\"\")\n551\t\n552\t            # Recreate indexes\n553\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n554\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n555\t\n556\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n557\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n558\t\n559\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n560\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE bead_watch\")\n561\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE bead_watch_new RENAME TO bead_watch\")\n562\t\n563\t            await db.commit()\n564\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: bead_watch.comment_high_water default is now -1\")\n565\t        except Exception as e:\n566\t            await db.rollback()\n567\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water: {e}\")\n568\t            raise\n569\t\n570\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n571\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n572\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n573\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n574\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n575\t\n576\t    # Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n625\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n626\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n627\t\n628\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n629\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n630\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n631\t\n632\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n633\t        \"\"\"\n634\t        async with cur.fetchone())[0]\n642\t            async with db.execute(\n643\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n644\t            ) as cur:\n645\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n646\t\n647\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n648\t            await db.execute(\n649\t                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n650\t            )\n651\t            await db.execute(\n652\t                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n653\t            )\n654\t            await db.execute(\n655\t                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n656\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n671\t            )\n672\t            await db.execute(\n673\t                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n674\t            )\n675\t            await db.execute(\n676\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n677\t                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE         \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n688\t            )\n689\t            await db.commit()\n690\t\n691\t        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n692\t\n693\t    # Surface operations\n694\t    async def register_surface(\n695\t        self,\n696\t        session_id: str,\n697\t        surface_type: Build update dynamically based on which values are provided\n827\t            updates = []\n828\t            params = []\n829\t\n830\t            if intent_type is not None:\n831\t                updates.append(\"intent_type = ?\")\n832\t                params.append(intent_type)\n833\t\n834\t            if status is not None:\n835\t                updates.append(\"status = ?\")\n836\t                params.append(status)\n837\t                # Nothing to update\n847\t                return\n848\t\n849\t            params.append(intent_id)\n850\t            query = f\"UPDATE intents SET {', '.join(updates)} WHERE id = ?\"\n851\t            await db.execute(query, params)\n852\t            await db.commit()\n853\t\n854\t    async def update_intent_topic(\n855\t        self,\n856\t        intent_id: str,\n857\t        topic_id: str | None,\n858\t    ) -> None:\n859\t        \"\"\"Update intent's primary topic.\n860\t\n861\t        Args:\n862\t            intent_id: The intent ID to update\n863\t            topic_id: New topic ID (None = don't update)\n864\t        \"\"\"\n865\t        if topic_id is None:\n866\t            return\n867\t\n868\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n869\t            await db.execute(\n870\t                \"UPDATE intents SET topic_id = ? Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n1474\t            await db.execute(\n1475\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n1476\t                (intent_id, now),\n1477\t            )\n1478\t            if cols:\n1479\t                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n1480\t                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n1481\t                await db.execute(\n1482\t                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1483\t                    params,\n1484\t                )\n1485\t            await db.commit()\n1486\t\n1487\t    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n1488\t        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n1489\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1490\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1491\t            async with db.execute(\n1492\t                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n1493\t                (intent_id,),\n1494\t            ) as cursor:\n1495\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n1496\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n1497\t\n1498\t    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n1499\t        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n1500\t\n1501\t        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n1502\t        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1503\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1504\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1505\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1506\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1507\t\n1508\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1509\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1510\t        \"\"\"\n1511\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1512\t\n1513\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1514\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1515\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1516\t                if since is not None:\n1517\t                    query = (\n1518\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1519\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n1520\t                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1521\t                    )\n1522\t                    params: tuple = (since,)\n1523\t                else:\n1524\t                    query = (\n1525\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1526\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n1527\t                    )\n1528\t                    params = ()\n1529\t                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n1530\t                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n1531\t                if not values:\n1532\t                    continue\n1533\t                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n1534\t                result[stage] = {\n1535\t                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n1536\t                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n1537\t                    \"count\": len(values),\n1538\t                }\n1539\t        return result\n1540\t\n1541\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n1542\t        self,\n1543\t        session_id: str,\n1544\t        since: int | None = None,\n1545\t    ) -> dict:\n1546\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n1547\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n1548\t\n1549\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1550\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n1551\t\n1552\t            # Get signal counts by type\n1553\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n1554\t            async with db.execute(\n1555\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n1556\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n1557\t                   WHERE session_id = ? \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n1576\t            }\n1577\t\n1578\t    async def create_result_with_diff(\n1579\t        self,\n1580\t        intent_id: str,\n1581\t        topic_id: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_303090a7f8cf463a878099f8","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Fetch Strand: Lookup Intent\n2\t\n3\tThis document defines the fetch strategy for `intent_type: lookup` queries.\n4\t\n5\t## What We Fetch\n6\t\n7\tFor a lookup query, we need to find specific information:\n8\t\n9\t1. **Logs**: Recent log output from pods/services\n10\t2. **Events**: Kubernetes events for the namespace\n11\t3. **Pod status**: Current state of pods\n12\t4. **Config data**: Configuration files, env vars (if applicable)\n13\t\n14\t## Command Matrix\n15\t\n16\t```bash\n17\t# Pod logs (tail last 100 lines)\n18\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} logs -n ${NAMESPACE} ${POD_NAME} --tail=100\n19\t\n20\t# Events for namespace\n21\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get events -n ${NAMESPACE} --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'\n22\t\n23\t# Pod status\n24\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get pods -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t## Parallel Execution\n28\t\n29\tAll fetch sources run concurrently. Logs may take longer; stream them if possible.\n30\t\n31\tTimeout per source:\n32\t- Logs: 10 seconds (can be slow for large pods)\n33\t- Events: 5 seconds\n34\t- Pod status: 5 seconds\n35\t\n36\t## Result Structure\n37\t\n38\t```json\n39\t{\n40\t  \"logs\": {\n41\t    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n42\t    \"data\": {\n43\t      \"pod\": \"pod-name\",\n44\t      \"logs\": \"log output here\",\n45\t      \"line_count\": 100\n46\t    }\n47\t  },\n48\t  \"events\": {\n49\t    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n50\t    \"data\": [ /* event objects */ ]\n51\t  },\n52\t  \"pods\": {\n53\t    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n54\t    \"data\": [ /* pod objects */ ]\n55\t  },\n56\t  \"coverage\": {\n57\t    \"logs\": true,\n58\t    \"events\": true,\n59\t    \"pods\": true\n60\t  }\n61\t}\n62\t```\n63\t\n64\t## Streaming Support\n65\t\n66\tFor logs, prefer streaming the response line-by-line as they arrive from kubectl.\n67\tThis makes the lookup feel faster even when logs are large.\n68\t\n69\t## Context Expansion\n70\t\n71\tFor lookup queries, include these context fields if available:\n72\t\n73\t- **Pod context**: Which pod, namespace, container\n74\t- **Time context**: Recent timestamp from user query (\"last hour\", \"since 2pm\")\n75\t- **Filter context**: Error-only, warnings, specific keywords\n76\t\n77\tThe fetch layer is deterministic. No LLM calls here — just execute the command matrix and return structured data.\n78\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_79e0b1df26b84172b6eefcb3","session_id":"bfe99d56-4ae6-4594-91fc-e8def902ad88","uuid":"c6727d44-4c53-47ae-9d19-b5201060e7ff","timestamp":"2026-07-23T17:41:12.644Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore lookup intent structure"}

intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n51\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n52\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n53\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n54\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n55\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n56\t\n57\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n58\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n61\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n62\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n63\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n64\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n65\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n66\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n67\t\n68\t\n69\t@dataclass\n70\tclass RenderOutcome:\n71\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n72\t\n73\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n74\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n75\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n76\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t\n79\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n80\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n81\t    card_fallback: bool\n82\t    layout_bucket: str\n83\t\n84\t\n85\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n86\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n87\t\n88\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n89\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n90\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n91\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n206\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n207\t    value: Any = data\n208\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n209\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return value\n228\t    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n229\t        return str(value)\n230\t    try:\n231\t        return json.dumps(value)\n232\t    except Exception:\n233\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t        try:\n246\t            obj = json.loads(data)\n247\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n248\t            return [('value', data)]\n249\t\n250\t    # Handle array\n251\t    if isinstance(obj, list):\n252\t        rows = []\n253\t        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n254\t            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n255\t        return rows\n256\t\n257\t    # Handle object\n258\t    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n259\t        rows = []\n260\t        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n261\t            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n262\t        return rows\n263\t\n264\t    # Handle primitive\n265\t    return [('value', str(obj))]\n266\t\n267\t\n268\tdef render_fallback_card(\n269\t    summary: str | None = None,\n270\t    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n271\t    urgency: str | None = None,\n272\t) -> str:\n273\t    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of summary: Optional result summary\n283\t        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n284\t        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n285\t\n286\t    Returns:\n287\t        HTML string for the fallback card\n288\t    \"\"\"\n289\t    try:\n290\t        # Build fallback card HTML\n291\t        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n292\t\n293\t        # Header with icon and title\n294\t        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n295\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n296\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n297\t        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n298\t\n299\t        # Summary paragraph\n300\t        if summary:\n301\t            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n302\t\n303\t        # Key/value grid from data\n304\t        if data:\n305\t            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n306\t            if rows:\n307\t                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n308\t                for key, value in rows:\n309\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n310\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n311\t                card_parts.append(' quote=False)}</span>')\n316\t\n317\t        card_parts.append('</div>')\n318\t\n319\t        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n320\t\n321\t    except Exception as e:\n322\t        # Fallback render select_rendered_card(\n329\t    result_type: str,\n330\t    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n331\t    result_id: str,\n332\t    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n333\t    match_threshold: Optional[rendered_html].\n337\t\n338\t    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. Queries component_usage_patterns\n339\t    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n340\t    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n341\t    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n342\t    fallback rendering by the caller).\n343\t\n344\t    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n345\t    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n346\t    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n347\t\n348\t    Args:\n349\t        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n350\t        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n351\t        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n352\t        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n353\t        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n354\t        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n355\t\n356\t    Returns:\n357\t        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n358\t    \"\"\"\n359\t    lib = library or get_library()\n360\t\n361\t    # \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n67\t\n68\t## Confidence Threshold\n69\t\n70\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n71\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n72\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n73\t\n74\t## Status Intent Notes\n75\t\n76\t- Populated after component\n227\t-- selection to avoid repeated rendering for the same result.\n228\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS card_cache (\n229\t    result_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\n230\t    component_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n231\t    layout_bucket  TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t    rendered_html  TEXT NOT NULL,\n233\t    created_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,\n234\t    PRIMARY KEY (result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)\n235\t);\n236\t\n237\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_card_cache_result_id ON card_cache(result_id);\n238\t\"\"\"\n239\t\n240\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n241\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n242\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n243\t    \"router_ms\",\n244\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n245\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n246\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n247\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n248\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n249\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n250\t    \"stt_ms\",\n251\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n252\t)\n253\t\n254\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n255\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n256\t\n257\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n258\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n259\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n260\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n261\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n284\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n285\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n286\t\n287\t            # Create schema\n288\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n289\t            await db.commit()\n290\t\n291\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n292\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n293\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n294\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n295\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n296\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n297\t\n298\t    @staticmethod\n299\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n300\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n301\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n302\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n303\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n304\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n305\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n306\t            await db.execute(\n307\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n308\t            )\n309\t\n310\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n311\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n312\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n313\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n314\t\n315\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n316\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n317\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n318\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n319\t\n320\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n336\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n337\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n338\t            status_col = None\n339\t            for col in cols:\n340\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n341\t                    status_col = col\n342\t                    break\n343\t\n344\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n345\t        if not status_col:\n346\t            return\n347\t\n348\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n349\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n350\t        async with db.execute(\n351\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n352\t        ) as cur:\n353\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n354\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n355\t                # Already migrated\n356\t                return\n357\t\n358\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n359\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n360\t\n361\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n362\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n363\t\n364\t        try:\n365\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n366\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n367\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n368\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n369\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n370\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n371\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n372\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n373\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n374\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n375\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n376\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n377\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n378\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n379\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n380\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n381\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n382\t                )\n383\t            \"\"\")\n384\t\n385\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n386\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n387\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n388\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n389\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n390\t                FROM intents\n391\t            \"\"\")\n392\t\n393\t            # Recreate indexes\n394\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n395\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n396\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n397\t\n398\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n399\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n400\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n401\t\n402\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n403\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n404\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n405\t\n406\t            await db.commit()\n407\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n408\t\n409\t        except Exception as e:\n410\t            await db.rollback()\n411\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n412\t            raise\n413\t\n414\t    @staticmethod\n415\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n416\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n417\t\n418\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n419\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n420\t        \"\"\"\n421\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n422\t        async with db.execute(\n423\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n424\t        ) as cur:\n425\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n426\t            if not table_sql:\n427\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n428\t\n429\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n430\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n431\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n432\t                    # Already migrated\n433\t                    return\n434\t\n435\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n436\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n437\t\n438\t        # With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n506\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n507\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n508\t        \"\"\"\n509\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n510\t        async with db.execute(\n511\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n512\t        ) as cur:\n513\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n514\t            if not table_sql:\n515\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n516\t\n517\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n518\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n519\t                # Already migrated\n520\t                return\n521\t\n522\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n523\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n524\t\n525\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n526\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n527\t\n528\t        try:\n529\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n545\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n546\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n547\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n548\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n549\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n550\t            \"\"\")\n551\t\n552\t            # Recreate indexes\n553\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n554\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n555\t\n556\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n557\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n558\t\n559\t            # NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n1503\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n1504\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n1505\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n1506\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n1507\t\n1508\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n1509\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n1510\t        \"\"\"\n1511\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n1512\t\n1513\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n1514\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n1515\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n1516\t                if since is not None:\n1517\t                    query = (\n1518\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n1519\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n51\t\n52\tTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n53\t\n54\t## Routing utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n65\t- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n66\t- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n67\t\n68\t## Confidence Threshold\n69\t\n70\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n71\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n72\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n73\t\n74\t## Status Intent Notes\n75\t\n76\t- Always include pod restart count in status results.\n77\t\n78\tUrgency classification rules are spliced in from `prompts/urgency.md` at call time and are hot-reloadable independently of this Intent Router System Prompt\n\nYou are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile|clarification\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n- **clarification**: Low-confidence routing outcome requiring user input (meta-type, not dispatched)\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\n## Status Intent Notes\n\n- Always include pod restart count in status results.\n\nUrgency classification rules are spliced in from `prompts/urgency.md` at call time and are hot-reloadable independently of this file.\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\n","numLines":81,"startLine":1,"totalLines":81}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8c8b5439f8264183a5bfcc07","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Fetch Strand: Lookup Intent\n2\t\n3\tThis document defines the fetch strategy for `intent_type: lookup` queries.\n4\t\n5\t## What We Fetch\n6\t\n7\tFor a lookup query, we need to find specific information:\n8\t\n9\t1. **Logs**: Recent log output from pods/services\n10\t2. **Events**: Kubernetes events for the namespace\n11\t3. **Pod status**: Current state of pods\n12\t4. **Config data**: Configuration files, env vars (if applicable)\n13\t\n14\t## Command Matrix\n15\t\n16\t```bash\n17\t# Pod logs (tail last 100 lines)\n18\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} logs -n ${NAMESPACE} ${POD_NAME} --tail=100\n19\t\n20\t# Events for namespace\n21\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get events -n ${NAMESPACE} --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'\n22\t\n23\t# Pod status\n24\tkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get pods -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\t## Parallel Execution\n28\t\n29\tAll fetch sources run concurrently. Logs may take longer; stream them if possible.\n30\t\n31\tTimeout per source:\n32\t- Logs: 10 seconds (can be slow for large pods)\n33\t- Events: 5 seconds\n34\t- Pod status: 5 seconds\n35\t\n36\t## Result Structure\n37\t\n38\t```json\n39\t{\n40\t  \"logs\": {\n41\t    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n42\t    \"data\": {\n43\t      \"pod\": \"pod-name\",\n44\t      \"logs\": \"log output here\",\n45\t      \"line_count\": 100\n46\t    }\n47\t  },\n48\t  \"events\": {\n49\t    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n50\t    \"data\": [ /* event objects */ ]\n51\t  },\n52\t  \"pods\": {\n53\t    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n54\t    \"data\": [ /* pod objects */ ]\n55\t  },\n56\t  \"coverage\": {\n57\t    \"logs\": true,\n58\t    \"events\": true,\n59\t    \"pods\": true\n60\t  }\n61\t}\n62\t```\n63\t\n64\t## Streaming Support\n65\t\n66\tFor logs, prefer streaming the response line-by-line as they arrive from kubectl.\n67\tThis makes the lookup feel faster even when logs are large.\n68\t\n69\t## Context Expansion\n70\t\n71\tFor lookup queries, include these context fields if available:\n72\t\n73\t- **Pod context**: Which pod, namespace, container\n74\t- **Time context**: Recent timestamp from user query (\"last hour\", \"since 2pm\")\n75\t- **Filter context**: Error-only, warnings, specific keywords\n76\t\n77\tThe fetch layer is deterministic. No LLM calls here — just execute the command matrix and return structured data.\n78\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_52f7f0d6217344419a064ccb","session_id":"bfe99d56-4ae6-4594-91fc-e8def902ad88","uuid":"84ec4b94-13aa-4307-9ba7-9fd1862ad23f","timestamp":"2026-07-23T17:41:40.676Z","subagent_type":"general-purpose","task_description":"Check lookup implementation status"}

Capture\n308\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n309\t        # the dispatch itself.\n310\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n311\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n312\t\n313\t        try:\n314\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n315\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n316\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n317\t            else:\n318\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n319\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n320\t        except Exception:\n321\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n322\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n323\t            raise\n324\t\n325\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n326\t        return result\n327\t\n328\t    async def _persist_timings(\n329\t        self,\n330\t        intent_id: str,\n331\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n332\t    ) -> None:\n333\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings.
