Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=None,  # Not populated in current classification\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=None,  # Not populated in current classification\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n            }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n                fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n\n\ndef get_router(store=None) -> IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global intent router instance.\"\"\"\n    global _router\n    if _router is None:\n        _router = IntentRouter(store=store)\n    return _router\n","numLines":900,"startLine":1,"totalLines":900}}}

When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\n9\tthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\n10\tthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n11\t\n12\tScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\n13\tUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\n14\tthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n15\t``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n16\t(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\n17\tUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n18\t\"\"\"\n19\t\n20\timport html\n21\timport json\n22\timport re\n23\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n24\tfrom logging import getLogger\n25\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n26\t\n27\tfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n28\t\n29\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n30\t\n31\t# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n32\t# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\n33\tDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n34\t\n35\t# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\n36\tDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n37\t\n38\t# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n39\t# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n40\t_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n41\t\n42\t\n43\tdef derive_result_type(\n44\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n45\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n46\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n47\t) -> str:\n48\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n49\t\n50\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n51\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n52\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n53\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n54\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n55\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n56\t\n57\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n58\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n61\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n62\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n63\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n64\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n65\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n66\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n67\t\n68\t\n69\t@dataclass\n70\tclass RenderOutcome:\n71\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n72\t\n73\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n74\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n75\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n76\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t\n79\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n80\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n81\t    card_fallback: bool\n82\t    layout_bucket: str\n83\t\n84\t\n85\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n86\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n87\t\n88\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n89\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n90\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n91\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n92\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n93\t    \"\"\"\n94\t\n95\t    def __init__(\n96\t        self,\n97\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n98\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n99\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n100\t    ):\n101\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n102\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n103\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n104\t\n105\t    def render(\n106\t        self,\n107\t        result_id: str,\n108\t        result_type: str,\n109\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n110\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n112\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n113\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n114\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n115\t\n116\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n117\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n118\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n119\t        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n120\t        component DB.\n121\t        \"\"\"\n122\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n123\t\n124\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n125\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n126\t        )\n127\t        if component is None:\n128\t            logger.debug(\n129\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n130\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n131\t                result_type,\n132\t                self.match_threshold,\n133\t                result_id,\n134\t            )\n135\t            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n136\t            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n137\t                summary=summary,\n138\t                data=result_data,\n139\t                urgency=urgency,\n140\t            )\n141\t            return RenderOutcome(\n142\t                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n143\t                component_id=None,\n144\t                card_fallback=True,\n145\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n146\t            )\n147\t\n148\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n149\t\n150\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n151\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n152\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n153\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n154\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n155\t        self.library.cache_card(\n156\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n157\t        )\n158\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n159\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n160\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n161\t\n162\t        logger.info(\n163\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n164\t            result_id,\n165\t            component.id,\n166\t            component.version,\n167\t            result_type,\n168\t        )\n169\t        return RenderOutcome(\n170\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n171\t            component_id=component.id,\n172\t            card_fallback=False,\n173\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n174\t        )\n175\t\n176\t\n177\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n179\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n182\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n183\t\n184\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n185\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n186\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n206\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n207\t    value: Any = data\n208\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n209\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return value\n228\t    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n229\t        return str(value)\n230\t    try:\n231\t        return json.dumps(value)\n232\t    except Exception:\n233\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t        try:\n246\t            obj = json.loads(data)\n247\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n248\t            return [('value', data)]\n249\t\n250\t    # Handle array\n251\t    if isinstance(obj, list):\n252\t        rows = []\n253\t        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n254\t            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n255\t        return rows\n256\t\n257\t    # Handle object\n258\t    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n259\t        rows = []\n260\t        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n261\t            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n262\t        return rows\n263\t\n264\t    # Handle primitive\n265\t    return [('value', str(obj))]\n266\t\n267\t\n268\tdef render_fallback_card(\n269\t    summary: str | None = None,\n270\t    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n271\t    urgency: str | None = None,\n272\t) -> str:\n273\t    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n274\t\n275\t    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n276\t    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n277\t    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n278\t\n279\t    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n280\t\n281\t    Args:\n282\t        summary: Optional result summary\n283\t        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n284\t        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n285\t\n286\t    Returns:\n287\t        HTML string for the fallback card\n288\t    \"\"\"\n289\t    try:\n290\t        # Build fallback card HTML\n291\t        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n292\t\n293\t        # Header with icon and title\n294\t        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n295\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n296\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n297\t        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n298\t\n299\t        # Summary paragraph\n300\t        if summary:\n301\t            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n302\t\n303\t        # Key/value grid from data\n304\t        if data:\n305\t            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n306\t            if rows:\n307\t                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n308\t                for key, value in rows:\n309\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n310\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n311\t                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n312\t\n313\t        # Urgency badge\n314\t        if urgency:\n315\t            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n316\t\n317\t        card_parts.append('</div>')\n318\t\n319\t        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n320\t\n321\t    except Exception as e:\n322\t        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n323\t        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n324\t        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n325\t        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n326\t\n327\t\n328\tdef select_rendered_card(\n329\t    result_type: str,\n330\t    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n331\t    result_id: str,\n332\t    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n333\t    match_threshold: It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n346\t    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n347\t\n348\t    Args:\n349\t        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n350\t        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n351\t        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n352\t        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n353\t        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n354\t        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n355\t\n356\t    Returns:\n357\t        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n358\t    \"\"\"\n359\t    lib = library or get_library()\n360\t\n361\t    # {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_59f56d1850c84e3ea811bfb6","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"Deterministic, no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n2\t\n3\tOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n4\t*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n5\t``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\n6\tfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\n7\tthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\n8\tstreaming. It NEVER writes component definitions\n16\t(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\n17\tUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n18\t\"\"\"\n19\t\n20\timport html\n21\timport json\n22\timport re\n23\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n24\tfrom logging import getLogger\n25\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n26\t\n27\tfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n28\t\n29\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n30\t\n31\t# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n32\t# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\n33\tDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n34\t\n35\t# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\n36\tDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n37\t\n38\t# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n39\t# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n40\t_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n41\t\n42\t\n43\tdef derive_result_type(\n44\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n45\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n46\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n47\t) -> str:\n48\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n49\t\n50\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n51\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n52\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n53\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n54\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n55\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n56\t\n57\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n58\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n61\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n62\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n63\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n64\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n65\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n66\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n67\t\n68\t\n69\t@dataclass\n70\tclass RenderOutcome:\n71\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n72\t\n73\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n74\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n75\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n76\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t\n79\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n80\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n81\t    card_fallback: bool\n82\t    layout_bucket: str\n83\t\n84\t\n85\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n86\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n87\t\n88\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n89\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n90\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n91\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n92\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n93\t    \"\"\"\n94\t\n95\t    def __init__(\n96\t        self,\n97\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n98\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n99\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n100\t    ):\n101\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n102\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n103\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n104\t\n105\t    def render(\n106\t        self,\n107\t        result_id: str,\n108\t        result_type: str,\n109\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n110\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n112\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n113\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n114\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n115\t\n116\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n117\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n118\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n119\t        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n120\t        component DB.\n121\t        \"\"\"\n122\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n123\t\n124\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n125\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n126\t        )\n127\t        if component is None:\n128\t            logger.debug(\n129\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n130\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n131\t                result_type,\n132\t                self.match_threshold,\n133\t                result_id,\n134\t            )\n135\t            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n136\t            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n137\t                summary=summary,\n138\t                data=result_data,\n139\t                urgency=urgency,\n140\t            )\n141\t            return RenderOutcome(\n142\t                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n143\t                component_id=None,\n144\t                card_fallback=True,\n145\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n146\t            )\n147\t\n148\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n149\t\n150\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n151\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n152\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n153\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n154\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n155\t        self.library.cache_card(\n156\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n157\t        )\n158\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n159\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n160\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n161\t\n162\t        logger.info(\n163\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n164\t            result_id,\n165\t            component.id,\n166\t            component.version,\n167\t            result_type,\n168\t        )\n169\t        return RenderOutcome(\n170\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n171\t            component_id=component.id,\n172\t            card_fallback=False,\n173\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n174\t        )\n175\t\n176\t\n177\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n179\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n182\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n183\t\n184\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n185\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n186\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n206\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n207\t    value: Any = data\n208\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n209\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return value\n228\t    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n229\t        return str(value)\n230\t    try:\n231\t        return json.dumps(value)\n232\t    except Exception:\n233\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\ndef select_rendered_card(\n    result_type: str,\n    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n    result_id: str,\n    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic component selector: (result_type, result.data) → Optional[rendered_html].\n\n    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    # Query component_usage_patterns for highest match_score where result_type matches\n    component = lib.select_component_for_result_type(\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_threshold=match_threshold,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    # If no component matched or below threshold, return None (triggers fallback)\n    if component is None:\n        logger.debug(\n            \"select_rendered_card: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n            \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n            result_type,\n            match_threshold,\n            result_id,\n        )\n        return None\n\n    # On match: fill component template with result.data\n    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n    # Write to card_cache\n    lib.cache_card(\n        result_id=result_id,\n        component_id=component.id,\n        component_version=component.version,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n    )\n\n    # Record usage pattern (hot-path match is high confidence, so score=1.0)\n    lib.record_usage_pattern(\n        component_id=component.id,\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_score=1.0,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    logger.info(\n        \"select_rendered_card: rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n        result_id,\n        component.id,\n        component.version,\n        result_type,\n    )\n\n    return rendered_html\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":431,"startLine":1,"totalLines":431}}}

}\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile|clarification\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"lookup_kind\":
