classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\nUses LLM to classify intents by type and project, then routes:\n- task-profile intents → escalate strand (bead creation)\n- other intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\nfrom ..escalate.handler import EscalateRequest, escalate_intent\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\nfrom ..instrument.timings import DispatchTimings\nfrom ..render.hot_path import derive_result_type, get_renderer\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom ..fetch.commands import FetchRequest, FetchContext, IntentType as FetchIntentType, get_fetch_commands\nfrom ..fetch.orchestrator import execute_fetch\nfrom ..synthesize.strand import SynthesizeRequest, synthesize_intent\nfrom ..sse.broadcaster import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent, EventType\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None logger.info(\"Component library initialized\")\n\n    # Initialize hot-reload manager\n    _reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n    logger.info(\"Hot-reload manager initialized\")\n\n    # _ambient_monitor = get_ambient_monitor()\n    await _ambient_monitor.start()\n    logger.info(\"Ambient monitor started\")\n\n    # Initialize context warmer (Phase 3)\n    _context_warmer = telegram_fallback = get_telegram_fallback()\n        bridge_available = await telegram_fallback.check_bridge_available()\n        if bridge_available:\n            logger.info(f\"Telegram bridge reachable at {telegram_fallback.bridge_url}\")\n        else:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Telegram bridge unreachable at {telegram_fallback.bridge_url}. \"\n                f\"Telegram fallback will not be available.\"\n            )\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"Failed to check Telegram bridge reachability: {e}\")\n\n    # Initialize bead watcher\n    try:\n        _bead_watcher = BeadWatcher(_store, _surface_router)\n        await _bead_watcher.start()\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"Bead watcher failed to start: {e}\")\n        _bead_watcher = None\n\n    logger.info(\"aide-de-camp ready\")\n    yield\n\n    # Shutdown\n    logger.info(\"Shutting down aide-de-camp...\")\n    stop_background_refresh()\n    if _bead_watcher:\n        await _bead_watcher.stop()\n    if _background_processor:\n        await _background_processor.stop()\n    if _context_warmer:\n        await _context_warmer.stop()\n    if _ambient_monitor:\n        await _ambient_monitor.stop()\n    if _broadcaster:\n        await _broadcaster.stop()\n    if (aide-de-camp)\", version=read_version(), lifespan=lifespan)\n\n# Include test router\napp.include_router(test_router, prefix=\"/api/v1\", tags=[\"test\"])\n\n\nasync def get_store():\n    \"\"\"Get or initialize the global session store.\"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        _store = session_store_get_store(DB_PATH)\n        await _store.initialize()\n    return _store\n\n\n@app.get(\"/health\")\nasync def health_check():\n    \"\"\"Health check endpoint.\n\n    Returns overall service status and watcher liveness block (alive, last_tick_at,\n    tick_count, interval). Watcher alive is true only while the task is running\n    AND last_tick_at is within 2x the poll interval. If _bead_watcher is None\n    (failed start), watcher.alive is false.\n    \"\"\"\n    response = {\n        \"status\": \"ok\",\n        \"service\": \"adc-voice\",\n    }\n    if _bead_watcher is not None:\n        response[\"watcher\"] = _bead_watcher.health_snapshot()\n    else:\n        # Watcher failed to start or not initialized\n        response[\"watcher\"] = {\n            \"alive\": False,\n            \"last_tick_at\": None,\n            \"tick_count\": 0,\n            \"interval\": 0,\n        }\n    return response\n\n\n@app.get(\"/\")\nasync def serve_canvas():\n    \"\"\"\n    Serve the canvas HTML page (Phase 0 minimal surface).\n\n    This is the main entry point for the aide-de-camp interface.\n    \"\"\"\n    return FileResponse(CANVAS_PATH)\n\n\n@app.get(\"/canvas.js\")\nasync def serve_canvas_js():\n    \"\"\"Serve the canvas render helpers (createTopicCard + friends).\n\n    Loaded by index.html via ``<script src=\"/canvas.js\">``. Kept as a separate\n    module so the rendering logic is unit-testable headlessly\n    (``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js``) without spinning up a browser.\n    \"\"\"\n    return FileResponse(CANVAS_JS_PATH, media_type=\"application/javascript\")\n\n\n@app.websocket(\"/voice\")\nasync def voice_session(websocket: WebSocket):\n    \"\"\"\n    Voice session endpoint using OpenAI Realtime API.\n\n    Handles:\n    - WebRTC connection establishment\n    - Tool-as-trigger dispatch_intent calls\n    - Async result delivery for narration\n    - Surface switch events (audio-to-canvas continuity)\n    \"\"\"\n    await websocket.accept()\n\n    # Get session ID from query param or create new\n    session_id = websocket.query_params.get(\"session_id\") or str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    logger.info(f\"Voice session connecting: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Get API key from environment\n    api_key = os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\")\n    if not api_key:\n        await websocket.send_json({\n            \"type\": \"error\",\n            \"error\": \"OpenAI API key not configured\"\n        })\n        await websocket.close(code=1011, reason=\"API key missing\")\n        return\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"audio\")\n    surface_id = audio_surface_id\n    logger.info(f\"Registered audio surface: {surface_id}\")\n\n    # Load voice prompt\n    voice_prompt = load_voice_prompt(VOICE_PROMPT_PATH)\n\n    # Surface switch handler for audio-to-canvas continuity\n    async def on_surface_switch(surface_type: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle surface switch event (audio -> canvas).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Surface switch: {surface_type}\")\n        try:\n            result = await handle_surface_switch(surface_type, session_id, voice)\n            # Send acknowledgment to client\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n                \"surface\": surface_type,\n                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Memory extraction handler for turn completion\n    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n    if memory_handler:\n        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n        )\n\n        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n            description=(\n                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Run the session\n        await voice.run()\n\n    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e)\n            })\n        except:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Mark surface disconnected\n        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n\n\n@app.post(\"/router\")\nasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent router endpoint.\n\n    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n                        sse_data = {\n                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                        }\n                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data=sse_data,\n                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n                            )\n                        )\n                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n                        try:\n                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n                                intent_id,\n                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n                            )\n                        except Exception as te:\n                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\nasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n\n    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n    stay NULL.\n\n    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n    if not intent_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n        )\n\n    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n        value = request.get(name)\n        if value is not None:\n            fields[name] = int(value)\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    try:\n        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n        )\n\n    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\nasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per dispatch stage (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n\n    Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n    that has at least one captured sample. The latency-baseline bead consumes\n    the un-windowed store helper directly; this endpoint exposes the same\n    numbers over HTTP for the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist and on-demand\n    inspection. Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n    dispatches only.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via bf CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        utterance=utterance,\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        project_slug=project_slug,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n        context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = intent_id\n\n    try:\n        # Escalate to bead\n        result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {escalate_request.intent_id} to bead {result.bead_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": result.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n            \"status\": result.status,\n            \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\nasync def get_environment():\n    \"\"\"Return the discovered environment: all git repos and bead workspaces across all hosts.\"\"\"\n    from .environment.discovery import get_registry\n    import time\n    registry = get_registry()\n    if not registry:\n        return {\"error\": \"Registry not yet initialized\"}\n    entries = [\n        {\n            \"name\": e.name,\n            \"slug\": e.slug,\n            \"path\": str(e.path),\n            \"host\": e.host,\n            \"display_path\": e.display_path,\n            \"has_beads\": e.has_beads,\n            \"remote_url\": e.remote_url,\n            \"remote_name\": e.remote_name,\n        }\n        for e in sorted(registry.all_entries(), key=lambda x: (x.host or \"\", x.slug))\n    ]\n    summary = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"total_repos\": summary[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": summary[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": summary[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": summary[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": summary[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"last_scan_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n        \"repos\": entries,\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/registry\")\nasync def get_registry_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Return the merged project registry (config/registry.yaml + discovery).\n\n    This is the DB-independent data source for the first-run welcome card (the\n    built-in family #2 — see plan, Component Library → Built-in cards, and Cold\n    start & demo seed). The welcome card renders even against an empty\n    components.db because the project list comes from this YAML-backed registry,\n    not the component DB. Returns only the welcome-relevant fields per project\n    (slug, name, description, intent_support, aliases) so no internal paths leak\n    to the served frontend.\n    \"\"\"\n    reg = get_yaml_registry()\n    projects = []\n    for slug, entry in (reg.get(\"projects\") or {}).items():\n        projects.append({\n            \"slug\": slug,\n            \"name\": slug,\n            \"description\": entry.get(\"description\") or \"\",\n            \"intent_support\": entry.get(\"intent_support\") or [],\n            \"aliases\": entry.get(\"aliases\") or [],\n        })\n    return {\"projects\": projects}\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/environment/refresh\")\nasync def trigger_environment_refresh():\n    \"\"\"Trigger an immediate environment rescan to discover new repos.\"\"\"\n    import time\n    registry = await refresh_registry()\n    s = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"status\": \"refreshed\",\n        \"total_repos\": s[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": s[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": s[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": s[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": s[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"scanned_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# API v1 Endpoints\n# =============================================================================\n\n# Pydantic models for API requests/responses\nclass SurfaceRegisterRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n\n\nclass HeartbeatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n\n\nclass FeedbackRequestModel(BaseModel):\n    feedback: str\n    feedback_type: str = \"self_modification\"\n    context: Optional[dict] = None\n    session_id: Optional[str] = None\n    require_approval: bool = True\n\n\nclass ApprovalRequest(BaseModel):\n    approval_id: str\n\n\nclass RollbackRequest(BaseModel):\n    artifact_name: str\n    artifact_type: str\n\n\nclass ComponentCreateRequest(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    description: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str = \"Initial version\"\n\n\nclass ComponentUpdateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    html_template: str\n    change_note: str\n\n\nclass ComponentIterateRequest(BaseModel):\n    component_id: str\n    feedback: str\n    result_data: Optional[dict] = None\n\n\nclass UsagePatternRecord(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"Request body for recording component usage patterns.\"\"\"\n    component_id: str\n    result_type: str\n    match_score: float\n    layout_bucket: str = \"normal\"\n\n\n# Canvas and Surface endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics\")\nasync def api_v1_get_session_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"Get active topic cards for a session (canvas session_id: str,\n    surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    surface_type: str = \"canvas\"\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        logger.info(f\"Registered {surface_type} surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n           yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",\n        },\n    )\n\n\n# Feedback and Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve and apply a pending change.\"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not Logs may take longer; stream them if possible.\n\nTimeout per source:\n- Logs: 10 seconds (can be slow for large pods)\n- Events: 5 seconds\n- Pod status: 5 seconds\n\n## Result Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"logs\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": {\n      \"pod\": \"pod-name\",\n      \"logs\": \"log output here\",\n      \"line_count\": 100\n    }\n  },\n  \"events\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* event objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"pods\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* pod objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"logs\": true,\n    \"events\": true,\n    \"pods\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Streaming Support\n\nFor logs, prefer streaming the response line-by-line as they arrive from kubectl.\nThis makes the lookup feel faster even when logs are large.\n\n## Context Expansion\n\nFor logs lookup queries, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **Pod context**: Which pod, namespace, container\n- **Time context**: Recent timestamp from user query (\"last hour\", \"since 2pm\")\n- **Filter context**: Error-only, warnings, specific keywords\n\nThe fetch layer is deterministic. Fetch Strand: Lookup Intent - Config Kind\n\nThis document defines the fetch strategy for `intent_type: lookup` with `lookup_kind: config`.\n\n## What We Fetch\n\nFor a config lookup query, we need configuration and deployment state:\n\n1. **ArgoCD app state**: Current sync status, health, and deployment info\n2. **Kubernetes resources**: Deployments, services, configmaps, secrets (metadata only)\n3. **Pod status**: Current pod state for running workloads\n4. **Git status**: Recent commits to declarative-config (if applicable)\n\n## Command Matrix\n\n```bash\n# ArgoCD application status\ncurl -s ${ARGOCD_PROXY}/api/v1/applications/${APP_NAME}\n\n# Deployment resources\nkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get deployment -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\n\n# Service resources\nkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get service -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\n\n# ConfigMap metadata\nkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get configmap -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\n\n# Pod status (for workload state)\nkubectl --server=${KUBECONTROL_PROXY} get pods -n ${NAMESPACE} -o json\n\n# Git log for declarative-config (if repo_path available)\ngit -C ${REPO_PATH} log -10 --oneline --pretty=format:'%h|%s|%an|%ar'\n```\n\n## Parallel Execution\n\nAll fetch sources run concurrently.\n\nTimeout per source:\n- ArgoCD app: 5 seconds\n- Deployments: 5 seconds\n- Services: 3 seconds\n- ConfigMaps: 3 seconds\n- Pods: 5 seconds\n- Git log: 3 seconds\n\n## Result Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"argocd_app\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": {\n      \"name\": \"app-name\",\n      \"sync\": {\n        \"status\": \"Synced|OutOfSync\",\n        \"revision\": \"main@sha256:...\"\n      },\n      \"health\": {\n        \"status\": \"Healthy|Degraded\",\n },\n  \"configmaps\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* configmap objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"pods\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* pod objects */ ]\n  },\n  \"git_log\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* commit entries */ ]\n  },\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"argocd_app\": true,\n    \"deployments\": true,\n    \"services\": true,\n    \"configmaps\": true,\n    \"pods\": true,\n    \"git_log\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Context Expansion\n\nFor config lookup queries, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **App context**: Which ArgoCD application, namespace\n- **Resource type**: Which specific resources (deployment, service, configmap)\n- **Time context**: \"current state\" vs historical\n- **Scope**: Cluster-wide vs namespace-specific\n\nThe fetch layer is deterministic. documentation and project overview information:\n\n1. **README**: Project README file\n2. **File structure**: Directory listing of the project\n3. **Documentation files**: Any docs/*.md or similar documentation\n4. **Package/service info**: Cargo.toml, package.json, or similar metadata files\n\n## Command Matrix\n\n```bash\n# README file\ncat ${REPO_PATH}/README.md\n\n# Directory listing (file structure)\nls -la ${REPO_PATH}\n\n# Documentation files (if docs/ directory exists)\nfind ${REPO_PATH}/docs -name \"*.md\" 2>/dev/null | head -20\n\n# Package metadata (language-specific)\n# For Rust projects:\ncat ${REPO_PATH}/Cargo.toml 2>/dev/null\n# For Python projects:\ncat ${REPO_PATH}/pyproject.toml 2>/dev/null\n# For Node projects:\ncat ${REPO_PATH}/package.json 2>/dev/null\n\n# Recent activity (for context)\ngit -C ${REPO_PATH} log -5 --oneline --pretty=format:'%h|%s|%an|%ar'\n```\n\n## Parallel Execution\n\nAll fetch sources run concurrently.\n\nTimeout per source:\n- README: 2 seconds\n- Directory listing: 2 seconds\n- Documentation files: 3 seconds\n- Package metadata: 2 seconds\n- Git log: 3 seconds\n\n## Result Structure\n\n```json\n{\n  \"readme\": {\n    \"status\": \"data\": {\n      \"type\": \"Cargo.toml|pyproject.toml|package.json\",\n      \"content\": { /* parsed metadata */ }\n    }\n  },\n  \"git_log\": {\n    \"status\": \"success|timeout|error\",\n    \"data\": [ /* recent commit entries */ ]\n  },\n  \"coverage\": {\n    \"readme\": true,\n    \"file_structure\": true,\n    \"documentation\": true,\n    \"package_metadata\": true,\n    \"git_log\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Context Expansion\n\nFor docs lookup queries, include these context fields if available:\n\n- **Project context**: Which project/repository\n- **Doc type**: README, API docs, architecture docs\n- **Specific file**: If user asks for a specific doc file\n- **Scope**: Overview vs detailed documentation\n\nThe fetch layer is deterministic.
